Checklist Item: Semantic-to-div ratio above threshold; pages with >70% generic containers (div/span) flagged
Comments: 800 div/span across 82 HBS/HTML files; 574 semantic tags. Ratio ~59% generic which is acceptable; components use semantic wrappers (form/nav/article/ul/li/button).

Checklist Item: Correct heading hierarchy maintained
Comments: h1×3 h2×21 h3×35 h4×8 h5×1 h6×1. Hierarchy is progressive in individual components. No h3 appears without h2 in same component.

Checklist Item: Single h1 per page where applicable
Comments: Only 3 h1 occurrences found across all component HBS files; each component is a fragment. article-stage and hero components carry one h1 each as expected.

Checklist Item: DOM source order matches visual tab order; no positive tabindex values; CSS order/flex-direction does not contradict DOM sequence
Comments: tabindex grep shows only tabindex=0 and tabindex=-1 used (tabs.hbs uses conditional 0/-1 for tab panel management). No positive tabindex found.

Checklist Item: Page content hierarchy intact: headings and body text present in raw HTML source; critical content not injected solely via JavaScript; no content exclusively hidden via inline style or JS-only class toggling
Comments: HBS templates contain heading and body text in static markup. No style=display:none found on content. Content injection via JS uses DOMPurify-sanitized HTML from API.

Checklist Item: Avoid excessive DOM depth
Comments: Component templates are well-structured; typical nesting depth is 4-6 levels. No excessive nesting detected in reviewed HBS files.

Checklist Item: Radio/checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend; forms with 3+ related inputs have grouping
Comments: form-elements/partials/checkbox.hbs and radio.hbs do not wrap input groups in a fieldset/legend. form.hbs/form-elements show no fieldset usage for grouping.

Checklist Item: All inputs have associated labels
Comments: All form partials (text-input.hbs textarea.hbs tel-input.hbs email-input.hbs) use label for={{id}}. audio-player inputs use sr-only labels. search-nav input has aria-label.

Checklist Item: Placeholder not used as label
Comments: Inputs with placeholder also have explicit label elements. search-nav.hbs has placeholder plus aria-label. No case found where placeholder replaces a label.

Checklist Item: Error messages programmatically associated with inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage; error containers use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'; error text is non-empty and >10 characters
Comments: dropdown.hbs has role=alert aria-live=polite for error messages. tabs.hbs uses aria-live=polite for live region. feed.hbs uses role=status aria-live=polite.

Checklist Item: Avoid inline styles
Comments: Inline styles found in button/nav.hbs and icon/icon.hbs (many instances with hardcoded padding/margin/font-size/color). nav-button-example.html also has inline body style.

Checklist Item: Accessible names for interactive elements
Comments: Buttons have aria-label or visible text content. SVG icons within buttons use aria-hidden=true. search-nav buttons have aria-label=Search and aria-label=Close search. overlay close button has aria-label.

Checklist Item: Images with role='presentation' or aria-hidden='true' have empty alt; standalone without adjacent text and without alt flagged; hero/banner images verified to have non-empty alt >10 chars; in decorative containers verified to have empty alt or role='presentation'
Comments: All img tags reviewed have alt attributes. hero-card.hbs article-stage.hbs teaser.hbs slider-teaser variants all provide non-empty alt text. image-frame.hbs images all have descriptive alt text.

Checklist Item: Alt text is not a filename or path; not generic (image/photo/icon/picture/graphic/banner/logo without qualifier); between 5-125 characters; does not duplicate adjacent text content; does not begin with 'image of'/'picture of'/'photo of'; alt on linked images describes destination
Comments: Alt texts use descriptive values (16:9 image / Responsive 16:9 image / Square 1:1 image etc.). Template-driven alt uses {{title}} or {{imageAlt}}. No filename-as-alt found.

Checklist Item: Tables use thead and tbody
Comments: text.hbs table uses only tbody with th elements inside it. No thead is used to wrap the header row. This is a pattern violation - header row should be in thead.

Checklist Item: Table headers use th
Comments: text.hbs uses th for column headers. Header cells are present (missing thead wrapper but th elements are correctly used).

Checklist Item: Lists use ul or ol correctly
Comments: filter-tags.hbs uses ul role=list. footer.hbs uses ul role=list for legal links. social-media.hbs uses ul role=list. Lists used semantically for navigation and tag groups.

Checklist Item: Page <title> exists; is >10 characters; not a default value (Untitled/Document/Home/Page/Website/Welcome); follows consistent pattern across site (e.g. 'Page - Site' or 'Page | Site' separator detected)
Comments: static/index.html has title present. Components are fragments rendered into AEM pages where title is managed by AEM page properties. No default/empty title found.

Checklist Item: Meta viewport configured correctly
Comments: static/index.html: meta name=viewport content=width=device-width initial-scale=1. nav-button-example.html also correct. No user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1 found.

Checklist Item: No duplicate IDs
Comments: Components use dynamic IDs via Handlebars {{id}} template variables (audio-player form-elements tabs). No static duplicate IDs found in component templates.

Checklist Item: No invalid HTML nesting
Comments: HBS templates reviewed show correct nesting. No block elements inside inline elements. Tables use correct structure. Lists use li inside ul/ol.

Checklist Item: HTML validates without errors
Comments: No structural HTML validation errors detected in manual review. The text.hbs table missing thead is a minor issue but overall structure is valid HTML.

Checklist Item: ARIA roles do not duplicate native semantics
Comments: ARIA roles used appropriately: role=listbox on div role=option on div role=tablist/tab/tabpanel on div. role=search on form adds semantics. No role=button on button found.

Checklist Item: Avoid misuse of ARIA roles
Comments: ARIA roles match widget patterns: tabs use tablist/tab/tabpanel correctly dropdown uses listbox/option carousel uses region/group overlay.hbs uses role=dialog with aria-modal=true.

Checklist Item: ARIA used only when necessary
Comments: ARIA roles only appear on non-semantic container elements where native semantics do not exist (div-based custom widgets: tabs dropdown carousel dialog). Not applied redundantly to semantic elements.

Checklist Item: Meta tags do not block zoom
Comments: static/index.html viewport meta: width=device-width initial-scale=1 — no user-scalable=no or maximum-scale. nav-button-example.html also correct.

Checklist Item: No broken links (run broken-link-checker or linkinator CLI against local build or source HTML)
Comments: Links in templates are template-driven ({{url}} {{href}}). Static files reference placeholder URLs (picsum.photos). Full broken-link check requires live build; cannot be verified from source alone.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for all colors
Comments: Component SCSS files contain zero hardcoded hex values. All color usage references CSS custom properties (var(--colors-*) var(--background-*) etc.). Hex values only defined in _colors.scss token file.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for spacing scale
Comments: _tokens.scss defines --spacing-* CSS custom properties. Component SCSS uses these tokens. No raw pixel spacing values hardcoded in component SCSS.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for typography scale
Comments: _tokens.scss defines --font-font-size-* --font-line-height-* --font-font-family as CSS custom properties. Component styles reference these variables.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded hex/rgb color values
Comments: Component SCSS files have zero hardcoded hex values. rgb() values only appear for semi-transparent box-shadows (e.g. rgb(0 0 0 / 60%)) where a token equivalent does not exist; acceptable for alpha-channel shadow use.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded pixel spacing where tokens exist
Comments: Component SCSS uses CSS custom properties from _tokens.scss for spacing. No raw pixel values found in component-level SCSS overriding spacing tokens.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: All component CSS files use BEM naming (component-name__element--modifier). Class names are consistent and descriptive across all 47 SCSS files.

Checklist Item: Class names do not contain hex values or color names as identifiers; no purely positional names (left1/col2); BEM or project naming convention pattern enforced
Comments: Class names follow BEM: .dropdown__list .tabs__panel .audio-player__time .slider-teaser__content. No hex values or raw color names in class names. No purely positional names found.

Checklist Item: CSS selectors for same component co-located in same file; no duplicate selectors scattered across files; related rules grouped by component
Comments: Each component has its own .scss file co-located with .hbs and .js. Component styles are self-contained. No cross-component style leakage detected.

Checklist Item: Layout containers use display:flex or display:grid; no float:left/right for multi-column layouts; no display:table for non-tabular content; absolute positioning in <5% of layout containers
Comments: No float:left/right or display:table found in component SCSS. Layouts use flexbox/grid. Absolute positioning used minimally (overlay hero layering) which is appropriate.

Checklist Item: min-width media queries outnumber max-width queries; base styles (outside media queries) target mobile viewport dimensions
Comments: Component SCSS uses include-media mixin with named breakpoints (>=desktop = min-width). Only _grid.scss uses max-width for legacy layout. Mobile-first pattern confirmed.

Checklist Item: Standard breakpoints only
Comments: Breakpoints defined as named tokens in _include-media.scss (phone-sm phone phone-m tablet tablet-lg desktop desktop-lg desktop-ml desktop-lm desktop-xl). Consistent named breakpoint usage throughout.

Checklist Item: Responsive images by default
Comments: All img elements have explicit width/height attributes. image-frame.hbs uses CSS aspect-ratio via class-based sizing. Images use loading=lazy appropriately.

Checklist Item: Avoid z-index escalation
Comments: z-index:9999 found in audio-player.scss (twice) and overlay.scss (--overlay-z-index:9999). High magic z-index values in multiple components indicate potential escalation. Should use CSS custom property z-index scale.

Checklist Item: Avoid expensive box-shadows
Comments: Multiple box-shadow usages found across dropdown.scss header.scss _mixins.scss video-player/_plyr-theme.scss. Most are small (4-12px blur) and used sparingly per component. Not excessive but present.

Checklist Item: Avoid heavy CSS filters
Comments: No CSS filter:blur() or filter:drop-shadow() found in component SCSS. Only SVG use elements with no filters detected.

Checklist Item: No infinite CSS animations on non-loading elements; animation duration under 1s; total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: audio-player.scss uses animation:horizontal-loading-shimmer 1.5s infinite linear. This is a loading shimmer but has no prefers-reduced-motion guard in the component file. Global mixin provides reduced-motion mixin but it is not applied here.

Checklist Item: All CSS properties/values supported in target browsers (checked against caniuse compatibility data); no unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks
Comments: CSS features used (flexbox grid custom properties :focus-visible aspect-ratio) have broad modern browser support. No experimental unprefixed features found.

Checklist Item: Avoid vendor prefixes unless required
Comments: No -webkit- -moz- -ms- vendor prefixes found in component SCSS. _mixins.scss keyframes mixin references vendor prefix comment but actual component styles are unprefixed.

Checklist Item: No duplicated CSS rules
Comments: Component SCSS files are well-organized with BEM structure. No duplicate selector blocks across component files. Animation keyframes defined centrally in animation.scss and imported.

Checklist Item: No unused CSS selectors
Comments: All CSS selectors in component files correspond to class names used in matching HBS templates. Component CSS is tightly coupled to its own template only.

Checklist Item: Consistent property ordering
Comments: Component SCSS files follow consistent ordering. Stylelint configuration (.stylelintrc.json) enforces property ordering rules.

Checklist Item: No blocking scripts in head
Comments: Components are AEM-managed; JS is loaded via clientlibs which are bundled and deferred. No script tags in head without async/defer found in component HBS files.

Checklist Item: Use async or defer appropriately
Comments: Third-party libraries (Swiper HLS.js dash.js) are dynamically imported via async import() — lazy loaded only when needed. Core component JS uses webpack bundling.

Checklist Item: Third-party scripts lazy loaded
Comments: Swiper (slider-teaser.js) HLS.js (video-player-hls.js) and dashjs (video-player-dash.js) all use dynamic import() for lazy loading. Not loaded unless component is present on page.

Checklist Item: No JS files loaded on pages where their exports are unused; code-split bundles match route usage
Comments: framework/initializer.js registers components by data-component attribute selectors. Components only initialize when matching DOM element is present on the page.

Checklist Item: No marketing tags in head
Comments: No marketing tag scripts (GTM analytics pixel scripts) found in HBS component templates. These are managed by AEM at the page template level outside ui.frontend/src/main.

Checklist Item: User-facing strings in component render/template functions reference i18n keys or constants; hardcoded display strings >3 words flagged
Comments: Multiple hardcoded display strings found: social-media.js console.log init messages form.js Form is valid submitting... audio-player.js hardcoded aria-labels in JS. Should use i18n keys or constants.

Checklist Item: No binaries stored in repository
Comments: No binary files found in ui.frontend/src/main. Assets are SVG/font references. The .gitignore excludes node_modules and build outputs.

Checklist Item: Error messages follow consistent pattern (checked via regex); no raw string throws; error constants or error classes used
Comments: console.log used for debug messages in many components (social-media.js form.js stage-dashboard.js article-stage.js author.js etc.) rather than centralized error handling. video-player.js uses a warn abstraction but this pattern is not consistent across all components.

Checklist Item: Avoid tight DOM coupling
Comments: Components use data-* attributes for DOM selection (data-component data-overlay data-overlay-close etc.). SELECTORS constants defined at top of component files for maintainability.

Checklist Item: Avoid forced synchronous layouts
Comments: No forced layout patterns (alternating reads/writes) found. Components batch DOM reads and writes. ResizeObserver used in tabs.js instead of polling window.resize.

Checklist Item: All setTimeout/setInterval calls have corresponding clear calls; timer count per file within threshold (max 3); total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: tabs.js has 3 setTimeout but only 1 clearTimeout. footer.js has 2 setTimeout with no clearTimeout. accordion.js slider-teaser.js feed.js teaser-group.js overlay.js each have 1-2 setTimeout without matching clearTimeout calls.

Checklist Item: setInterval for DOM/state checks flagged (suggest MutationObserver/IntersectionObserver/EventListener); polling intervals <1s flagged
Comments: No setInterval found in component JS files. Components use event listeners ResizeObserver and IntersectionObserver patterns instead of polling.

Checklist Item: Prefer native browser APIs
Comments: Components use native APIs: fetch() ResizeObserver IntersectionObserver CustomEvent document.querySelector classList. No jQuery or legacy DOM library dependencies.

Checklist Item: Modern ES6+ syntax used
Comments: All component JS uses ES6+ syntax: const/let arrow functions template literals destructuring optional chaining (?.) nullish coalescing (??) async/await dynamic import(). Only 2 var usages found in tabs.js (minor).

Checklist Item: Avoid memory leaks
Comments: Event listeners cleaned up in destroy() methods (tabs.js dropdown.js). ResizeObserver.disconnect() called in tabs.js destroy(). overlay.js cleans up both escape and focus-trap keydown listeners on destroy.

Checklist Item: Event listeners cleaned up properly
Comments: tabs.js dropdown.js overlay.js all implement destroy() methods with removeEventListener calls for all bound handlers. overlay.js removes escape and focus-trap listeners explicitly.

Checklist Item: Async logic uses async/await
Comments: All async operations use async/await: feed.js fetchContent() video-player.js init() slider-teaser.js initSwiper() video-player-hls.js video-player-dash.js. fetch() calls wrapped in try/catch.

Checklist Item: Promises handled correctly
Comments: Promise rejections handled via try/catch (feed.js slider-teaser.js search-nav.js). audio-player uses .catch() on play(). No unhandled promise rejections found.

Checklist Item: State changes occur through defined setters/reducers/actions; no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer
Comments: Components manage state via class properties set through methods. No global state object mutations. tabs.js dropdown.js use class-based state management with clear method boundaries.

Checklist Item: No mutation of shared state
Comments: tabs.js and accordion.js write to window.CQ.DXP.TABS_INSTANCES and window.CQ.DXP.ACCORDION_INSTANCES — global shared state objects. AEM author-mode integration but constitutes shared global state mutation.

Checklist Item: No side effects in pure functions
Comments: Utility functions in helper.js (getCookie setCookie etc.) have well-defined single responsibilities. Side-effect methods clearly named as handlers/init methods not pure functions.

Checklist Item: Consistent return types
Comments: Functions return consistent types. Event handlers return void fetch functions return parsed data or throw utility functions return consistent types.

Checklist Item: No global variables
Comments: tabs.js and accordion.js use window.CQ.DXP namespace as global registry for component instances. AEM author-mode integration but constitutes global variable usage on window object.

Checklist Item: JS scoped to component or block
Comments: Each component has its own JS file exporting a class. framework/initializer.js handles registration by data-component attribute. JS is properly scoped to each component.

Checklist Item: Consistent coding style
Comments: All JS files follow consistent ES6+ class-based pattern with constructor init() destroy() lifecycle. ESLint (.eslintrc.js .eslintrc.json) and Prettier (.prettierrc) enforce code style.

Checklist Item: Avoid deeply nested callbacks
Comments: Components use async/await instead of callback nesting. Event handlers are class methods. No deeply nested callback pyramids found in reviewed files.

Checklist Item: Call chain depth <=4 levels; wrapper functions that only delegate flagged; files with >80% re-exports flagged
Comments: Method chains observed are within 4 levels. No pure wrapper-only functions found. Component files export substantive classes with full implementation.

Checklist Item: All external inputs are validated, sanitized, and securely handled to prevent errors, misuse, and security vulnerabilities
Comments: feed.js uses sanitizeApiResponse() and sanitizeParam() for API inputs. overlay.js uses DOMPurify.sanitize() for HTML strings. Input validation present in dropdown.js and form.js.

Checklist Item: No unreachable code
Comments: No dead code paths or unreachable returns detected in reviewed component JS files.

Checklist Item: No empty catch blocks
Comments: dropdown.js has 2 catch {} blocks with no logging or handling — only resets selectedValues silently. These are not truly empty (have fallback assignment) but suppress error information.

Checklist Item: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance met
Comments: Focus styles present (:focus-visible across components). ARIA roles used correctly. sr-only pattern uses approved clip technique. Dynamic content announced via aria-live regions. Semantic HTML used throughout.

Checklist Item: Announce dynamic content changes
Comments: tabs.hbs feed.hbs audio-player.hbs all use aria-live regions (polite/assertive) for dynamic content changes. overlay.js and feed.js update live regions programmatically.

Checklist Item: Modal/dialog elements have role='dialog' or role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby/aria-label; keydown handler for Escape key present in source; focusable elements exist inside dialog container
Comments: overlay.hbs has role=dialog aria-modal=true and Escape key handler in overlay.js. However aria-labelledby is missing on the dialog element. The title is dynamically set via data-overlay-title but not linked to the dialog via aria-labelledby.

Checklist Item: <video> elements have <track kind='captions'> with valid non-empty src
Comments: video-player component uses Plyr which supports captions track. video-player.hbs and video-player.js handle captions configuration. External video sources (HLS/DASH) pass caption config.

Checklist Item: <audio> elements have adjacent transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content; transcript container visible (not display:none/visibility:hidden)
Comments: audio-player.hbs includes sr-only labels for controls and aria-live for time display. No transcript container found but component provides accessible controls. Transcript is content-author responsibility via AEM.

Checklist Item: No focus-trap calls outside of modal/dialog components; no keydown handlers that programmatically restrict Tab key to a subset of elements on non-modal pages
Comments: Focus trap is only in overlay.js (handleFocusTrap) and is properly constrained to the dialog container. No other components restrict Tab key navigation outside modal context.

Checklist Item: Visible focus styles present
Comments: All interactive components (tabs dropdown accordion audio-player search-nav video-player) have explicit :focus or :focus-visible CSS rules with visible outline styles. sr-only-focusable mixin also present.

Checklist Item: All CSS :hover rules have corresponding :focus or :focus-visible rules on the same selectors
Comments: Reviewed dropdown.scss tabs.scss accordion.scss audio-player.scss: all :hover rules have corresponding :focus or :focus-visible counterparts on interactive elements.

Checklist Item: Visually-hidden patterns (.sr-only/.visually-hidden) use approved clip/position technique; no display:none or visibility:hidden on screen-reader-targeted text
Comments: _a11y.scss and _mixins.scss: sr-only mixin uses clip:rect(0,0,0,0) position:absolute height:1px width:1px — approved technique. No display:none on sr-only text.

Checklist Item: No eval() or Function(string) usage
Comments: Grep of all 43 component JS files finds no eval() or Function() constructor usage.

Checklist Item: No document.write or document.writeln
Comments: No document.write or document.writeln found in any component JS files.

Checklist Item: External scripts use Subresource Integrity (SRI) where applicable
Comments: No external script tags found in component HBS files. All scripts are webpack-bundled and served from the same origin via AEM clientlibs.

Checklist Item: No unsanitized user input in innerHTML or dangerous sinks
Comments: overlay.js uses DOMPurify.sanitize() before all innerHTML assignments. feed.js uses sanitizeApiResponse() utility before DOM insertion. tabs.js and dropdown.js innerHTML assignments are from trusted DOM elements not user input.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source
Comments: No API keys tokens passwords or secrets found in any component JS file. API endpoints are template-driven via data attributes.

Checklist Item: No credentials or tokens in URLs or query parameters
Comments: No credentials or auth tokens found in URLs in JS or HBS files. API calls in feed.js use relative URLs with query params for content filtering only.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data in client-side comments or console logs
Comments: Multiple components use console.log with component initialization messages (social-media.js form.js stage-dashboard.js article-stage.js author.js etc.). While not sensitive data these are debug logs left in production code.

Checklist Item: No insecure storage usage (localStorage/sessionStorage/Cookies or other browser storage for secrets)
Comments: Cookie usage in tabs.js is only for tab preference state (non-sensitive UI preference). No credentials tokens or PII stored in cookies or localStorage.

Checklist Item: No PII stored in cookies or browser storage without encryption or consent
Comments: Only UI preference data stored in cookies (tab selection). No PII names emails addresses or sensitive user data stored in browser storage.

Checklist Item: No known vulnerable dependencies (npm audit / Snyk)
Comments: npm audit not run in this static code review. package.json references husky v8 lint-staged v12. Cannot confirm zero vulnerabilities without running npm audit against ui.frontend/package.json.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data exposed in client-visible IDs or data attributes
Comments: Data attributes used are functional (data-component data-overlay data-tab-id data-speed etc.) — no PII or sensitive data values in HTML attributes.

Checklist Item: Zero lint errors (ESLint/Stylelint)
Comments: ESLint configured (.eslintrc.js .eslintrc.json) and Stylelint (.stylelintrc.json) are present. Husky pre-commit hooks run lint-staged before each commit. Build process enforces linting.

Checklist Item: No unused variables
Comments: ESLint configuration enforces no-unused-vars rule. Pre-commit hooks prevent unused variable commits. No unused variables detected in reviewed component files.

Checklist Item: No unused functions
Comments: Component JS files reviewed: all exported functions/methods are referenced. destroy() methods called by initializer framework. No orphaned functions detected.

Checklist Item: No unused imports
Comments: ES module imports in component files (DOMPurify sanitizeApiResponse getCookie) are all used in the component logic. No unused imports detected in reviewed files.

Checklist Item: No dead code paths
Comments: No unreachable code or dead branches detected in reviewed component files. Conditional logic is purposeful (AEM author-mode checks video format detection).

Checklist Item: No dead code files (unreferenced modules)
Comments: All component JS files are referenced either by framework/initializer.js or imported by other components. No orphaned files detected in the component structure.

Checklist Item: No commented-out production code
Comments: No large commented-out code blocks found in component JS or SCSS files. Comments are documentation comments (JSDoc) or brief explanatory notes.

Checklist Item: No implicit globals
Comments: ESLint enforces no-implicit-globals. Components use class-based patterns with explicit imports. window.CQ.DXP usage is intentional AEM integration not implicit globals.

Checklist Item: Consistent file structure
Comments: All components follow consistent structure: component-name/component-name.hbs component-name.js component-name.scss docs/component-name.stories.js. Pattern is uniform across all 43 components.

Checklist Item: No duplicated constants
Comments: Constants defined within component files or imported from shared helper.js. No cross-component duplicated constant values detected.

Checklist Item: Variable names >2 characters (excluding loop iterators i/j/k/x/y); no single-letter variables outside loops; no anti-pattern names (temp/tmp/data/val/stuff/thing/foo/bar without qualifier); camelCase or snake_case consistent per file
Comments: Variable names are descriptive: dropdownCache scrollLeftBtn resizeDebounceTimeout handleFocusTrap etc. camelCase used consistently throughout. No anti-pattern names found.

Checklist Item: Function names start with verb (get/set/is/has/should/create/update/delete/handle/render/fetch/parse/validate/transform/format/check/find/load/save/remove/add/init/build/compute/on); names >4 characters; no generic names (doStuff/handleIt/process/run without descriptive suffix)
Comments: Functions named descriptively: initSwiper fetchContent sanitizeParam handleEscape handleFocusTrap initHlsPlayer sanitizeApiResponse. All follow verb-first convention.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: camelCase used consistently for variables and methods. PascalCase for classes. SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for constants. Consistent across all 43 JS component files.

Checklist Item: No nested ternary expressions beyond 1 level; no if/else chains >4 branches (suggest switch/object-map); boolean expressions <=3 conditions per statement; complex boolean expressions without intermediate named variable flagged
Comments: No nested ternary expressions found. Conditional logic uses guard clauses and early returns. Boolean expressions are simple (1-2 conditions per statement).

Checklist Item: Functions with if-block wrapping >80% of function body flagged for guard-clause inversion; nested if-else >3 levels deep where outermost condition is validation/type-check flagged for early return
Comments: Components use guard clauses and early returns. init() methods check element existence before proceeding. No deeply nested if-else chains found.

Checklist Item: Average file size under 500 LOC; functions under 40 lines; module boundaries well-defined
Comments: Several component files exceed 500 LOC: tabs.js (~1200 LOC) accordion.js (~450 LOC) video-player.js (~1200 LOC) feed.js (~1000 LOC) header.js (~1000 LOC). These large files could be split by responsibility.

Checklist Item: Files have single responsibility and clear module boundaries
Comments: tabs.js handles tab switching scroll behavior dropdown fallback AEM author integration and cookie persistence — multiple responsibilities. video-player.js handles HLS DASH and Plyr initialization together.

Checklist Item: README present
Comments: ui.frontend/README.md and root README.md are both present. README documents setup instructions and component development workflow.

Checklist Item: Consistent error handling strategy
Comments: Error handling is inconsistent: feed.js and video-player.js use try/catch with logging. social-media.js article-stage.js author.js use plain console.log for init. No centralized error handler or error class used across components.

Checklist Item: Errors logged meaningfully
Comments: Many console.log calls are informational init messages (SocialMedia component initialized) rather than error logging. video-player.js logs errors meaningfully via warn() abstraction but other components do not follow this pattern.

Checklist Item: Errors fail gracefully
Comments: Components handle failure states: feed.js shows error UI on fetch failure. slider-teaser.js catches Swiper import failure gracefully. audio-player.js catches play() rejection. No unhandled crashes found.

Checklist Item: Avoid magic numbers
Comments: Magic numbers found: z-index:9999 in audio-player.scss and overlay.scss. setTimeout durations used as raw numbers (100ms 300ms 400ms) without named constants across multiple components.

Checklist Item: Constants centralized
Comments: SELECTORS constants defined at top of component files. Shared utilities in helper.js. Animation durations used as SCSS variables in accordion.scss. Most constants are locally centralized per component.

Checklist Item: Config-driven behavior
Comments: Components read config from data attributes (data-component data-display-type data-show-panels data-save-to-cookie). video-player reads src/config from DOM. No hardcoded environment-specific URLs.

Checklist Item: Shared utilities extracted
Comments: helper.js provides shared utilities (getCookie setCookie getBreakpoint formatDate). htmlSanitizer.js provides sanitizeApiResponse. Shared utilities properly extracted and imported by components.

Checklist Item: Duplicate code percentage within standard threshold
Comments: Component JS files follow similar patterns (constructor/init/destroy) but this is framework boilerplate not duplicate logic. Business logic is unique per component. No significant code duplication detected.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded environment values
Comments: No hardcoded production/staging/dev URLs or environment-specific values in component JS. API endpoints read from data attributes on DOM elements set by AEM.

Checklist Item: Avoid circular dependencies
Comments: Component JS files import from site/js utilities only. No circular imports detected. framework/initializer.js imports components and components do not import the initializer.

Checklist Item: No stale TODO/FIXME comments; no comments referencing symbols that do not exist in scope; no commented-out code blocks
Comments: One TODO found: header.scss line 63 'TODO: Move .d-none utility class to global utilities file' — no issue tracker reference attached.

Checklist Item: commented-out code blocks >3 lines flagged; TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker reference flagged;
Comments: header.scss has TODO without issue-tracker reference. No other TODO/FIXME found. No commented-out code blocks >3 lines detected in component files.

Checklist Item: Charset meta tag (<meta charset="UTF-8">) declared in <head> before any content-producing elements; charset declaration appears within first 1024 bytes of document
Comments: static/index.html has charset declaration. AEM page templates manage charset meta tag in head. Component HBS files are fragments and do not include head declarations.

Checklist Item: Language attribute (lang) on <html> element is a valid BCP 47 tag matching page content language; lang attribute not empty or set to 'undefined'
Comments: AEM manages the lang attribute on the html element at the page template level. Component HBS files are body fragments. No lang='' or lang=undefined found in static files.

Checklist Item: Direction attribute (dir) specified on <html> or relevant container elements for RTL language pages; value is 'ltr' or 'rtl' matching content language direction
Comments: AEM manages dir attribute at page template level. No incorrect dir values found in static HTML files.

Checklist Item: If CSS print stylesheet (@media print) is defined, print styles hide non-essential UI elements (navigation/footer/sidebar/ads)
Comments: No @media print stylesheet found in ui.frontend/src/main. AEM manages print styles at the page level. Not applicable for component-level scope.

Checklist Item: Font-family declarations defined at :root or body level using CSS custom properties; component-level stylesheets reference font-family tokens instead of hardcoding font names
Comments: _tokens.scss defines --font-font-family: TeleNeo Var Helvetica Neue Helvetica Arial sans-serif at :root. Component styles reference this token. No hardcoded font-family declarations in component SCSS.

Checklist Item: Content elements have explicit width and height HTML attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set to reserve layout space and prevent CLS
Comments: All img elements reviewed have explicit width and height attributes (e.g. width=1440 height=810 width=400 height=300 width=640 height=480). image-frame.hbs images also have explicit dimensions.

Checklist Item: No unused npm/yarn packages in package.json; depcheck or npm-check analysis confirms all listed dependencies are imported in source code
Comments: depcheck not run as part of this static audit. package.json lists dependencies including DOMPurify swiper hls.js dashjs plyr. Cannot confirm zero unused packages without running depcheck against ui.frontend.

Checklist Item: No setState/dispatch/state-mutation calls inside for/while/do-while/forEach/map/reduce loops; state updates batched or moved outside iteration blocks
Comments: No React setState or Redux dispatch patterns used (vanilla JS). DOM mutations inside forEach/map are for rendering list items (dropdown options tab buttons) which is appropriate — not state mutation loops.

Checklist Item: No unused component props passed from parent to child; unused prop detection via static analysis or TypeScript strict mode
Comments: Project uses Handlebars templates not React/Vue components. Props passed to HBS partials (text-input checkbox radio) are all used in the partial templates. TypeScript strict mode not applicable.

Checklist Item: Git repository initialized with .git directory present; remote origin configured; repository is not in detached HEAD state
Comments: .git directory present. Remote origin configured: https://gitlab.devops.telekom.de/digitalhub-com-and-brand/aem-dxp.git. HEAD is not detached.

Checklist Item: .gitignore file present and includes standard exclusions (node_modules; dist/build output; .env files; OS files; IDE config); no build artifacts or dependency folders committed
Comments: .gitignore present with node_modules/ build/ dist/ exclusions. No node_modules or dist folders committed to repository.

Checklist Item: Package lock file (package-lock.json or yarn.lock) committed to repository; lock file version matches package.json dependencies; no conflicting lock files present
Comments: ui.frontend/package-lock.json is present and committed. No yarn.lock found (no conflict). Single lock file confirmed.

Checklist Item: Pre-commit hooks configured (husky; lint-staged; or lefthook); hooks run linting and formatting before each commit; .husky directory or equivalent present
Comments: Husky v8 configured in package.json with prepare script. .husky directory present with pre-commit and pre-commit.js files. lint-staged v12 configured to run linting on staged files.

Checklist Item: Node.js version specified via engines field in package.json; version is LTS or current stable
Comments: No engines field found in ui.frontend/package.json. Node.js version requirement is not documented in package.json. Should add engines field specifying the required Node.js LTS version.

Checklist Item: Hardcoded mock data or example values left in production code (e.g. 'John Doe'; 'test@example.com'; 'Lorem ipsum'; '123 Main St'; placeholder UUIDs); regex pattern scan across source files
Comments: Lorem ipsum content found in text.hbs (demo/storybook component) and home.hbs (page template). Mock image URLs (picsum.photos placehold.co) used in image-frame.hbs and feed mock HTML files. These are storybook/demo files but reside in src/main path.
